title: Reserved Domains Reference Pack Recommendation
description: Recommend which public reference pack Golem should use when it needs to explain reserved, example and special-use DNS names in future notes or docs.
question: Which public reference pack should Golem use as the default baseline when it needs to explain reserved, example and special-use DNS names in future documentation or research notes?

sources.label: iana_reserved
sources.notes: Institutional overview of reserved and policy-special domains.

sources.label: rfc_2606
sources.notes: Best Current Practice defining reserved example names.

decision_criteria.label: Normative Authority
decision_criteria.description: The reference pack should include a primary standards-facing source.
decision_criteria.notes: Favor standards-facing material over short summaries.

decision_criteria.label: Example Name Coverage
decision_criteria.description: The reference pack should explicitly cover reserved example names and localhost/invalid behavior.
decision_criteria.notes: Coverage matters more than prose style.

decision_criteria.label: Special-Use Scope
decision_criteria.description: The reference pack should make special-use behavior and scope explicit, not only list examples.
decision_criteria.notes: Important if the pack is going to be reused in future notes.

decision_criteria.label: Operator Overview
decision_criteria.description: The reference pack should still help a reader quickly understand practical registry or documentation context.
decision_criteria.notes: Keeps the pack useful outside pure RFC reading.

alternatives.label: RFC 6761 Primary + IANA Secondary
alternatives.description: Use RFC 6761 as the main normative reference and IANA reserved domains as the operator-friendly companion.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Have one strong standards source plus one practical overview for future notes.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Medium relative cost because it requires carrying a standards document plus a practical overview, but both stay public and stable.
risk_hints: The RFC remains denser than a casual overview for new readers.
The pack can feel heavier than a single-page summary.
preconditions: Accept that the primary reference will be normative and somewhat longer.
Keep IANA reserved domains as the practical companion instead of relying on the RFC alone.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Use RFC 6761 as the primary cited baseline and pair it with IANA reserved domains whenever the project needs a lighter operator-facing explanation.
alternatives.notes: Best fit when the project needs durable technical grounding plus a readable companion page.

alternatives.label: IANA Primary + RFC 2606 Secondary
alternatives.description: Use the IANA reserved domains page as the main baseline and RFC 2606 as the standards-oriented backup.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Optimize for a quick operator overview with one lighter RFC behind it.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Low relative cost because the pack is lighter and easier to scan than a newer standards-heavy pairing.
risk_hints: Can underplay the full special-use scope compared with RFC 6761.
May leave follow-up questions once the topic goes beyond example names.
preconditions: Treat RFC 2606 as a fallback rather than the deepest source.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Use the IANA page when speed matters most, but escalate to RFC 6761 when the topic moves from examples to broader special-use behavior.
alternatives.notes: Best fit for a lighter baseline, not the deepest normative pack.

alternatives.label: RFC 2606 Primary + RFC 6761 Secondary
alternatives.description: Use RFC 2606 as the main reference and RFC 6761 as the supporting scope-expander.
alternatives.intended_outcome: Favor the classic example-name RFC and keep the broader special-use RFC nearby.
alternatives.relative_cost_note: Medium relative cost because it keeps two RFCs in play and is more standards-heavy than an IANA-led pack.
risk_hints: Can stay too focused on classic example names if the reader never opens RFC 6761.
Less operator-friendly than a pack that includes the IANA overview.
preconditions: Be ready to bring in RFC 6761 whenever special-use scope matters.
alternatives.suggested_next_step: Use this pack only when the project already knows it wants an RFC-first reading path and can tolerate lower operator readability.
alternatives.notes: Good as an RFC-centric pack, weaker as a broad default baseline.
